data.title: 3 Energy Sector Stocks Tied To Inflation And Hidden Cash Flows
data.description:
Energy prices are at the center of the latest inflation shock, and that ripple effect is touching everything from household fuel bills to corporate profit margins. With U.S. inflation at 4.2% and energy costs jumping sharply, some stocks tied to this theme may face new pressures, while others could see fresh interest from investors looking for exposure to the current pricing reality. This article highlights 3 large energy sector stocks from a targeted screener that are closely exposed to the recent news on inflation and the Iran war, and explains why each might deserve a closer look now.

Serica Energy (AIM:SQZ)

Overview: Serica Energy is a UK oil and gas producer that identifies, acquires, and develops offshore reserves, then produces and sells gas, oil, and natural gas liquids, with operations centered in the UK North Sea and management based in Aberdeen.

Operations: Serica generated about $601.4 million in revenue from oil and gas exploration, development, production and related activities, all from the UK market.

Market Cap: £1.0b

Serica Energy offers direct exposure to oil and gas prices influenced by geopolitical tensions, with the company already reporting about $601 million in annual revenue and expanding its footprint through the Greater Laggan Area acquisition. The stock combines this leverage to commodity prices with a sizeable UK North Sea position and a dividend policy that includes a proposed 10.0 pence final dividend, even after a recent year of losses. At the same time, investors need to weigh concentrated UK regulatory risk, production outages and share price volatility. Investors may also consider the potential impact of developments in the Iran conflict on energy markets, as well as Serica’s progress on its projects, when assessing any perceived gap between the current share price and the company’s long term cash flow potential.

Serica’s mix of UK North Sea scale, a proposed 10.0 pence dividend and sensitivity to geopolitics is only half the story. The 3 key rewards and 2 important warning signs (1 is major!) could change how you view that recent loss and future cash flows.

Athabasca Oil (TSX:ATH)

Overview: Athabasca Oil is a Canadian producer focused on thermal oil from the Athabasca oil sands and light oil and gas from the Duvernay region, giving it exposure to both heavy and lighter crude in Alberta’s Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin.

Operations: Athabasca Oil generates about CA$1.33b in revenue from its Thermal Oil segment and CA$77.0m from Duvernay Energy, with all reported revenue from Canada and a small consolidation adjustment of CA$70.6m.

Market Cap: CA$5.59b

Athabasca Oil is closely tied to movements in energy prices, with pure oil exposure through its Athabasca and Duvernay assets at a time when inflation is being influenced by more expensive fuel. The stock trades below one estimate of its future cash flow value and on a lower P/E than many peers. However, recent earnings and margins have softened, and returns on equity are 11.1%. At the same time, liquidity has been reinforced by new credit facilities and a net cash position, which may support future growth initiatives. The key consideration for you is how that mix of valuation, funding capacity and concentrated oil exposure fits with your own view once you see the full picture.

Athabasca Oil’s mix of pure oil exposure, softer margins and reinforced liquidity raises a simple question for you: is the current pricing masking the real story behind its 2 key rewards and 1 important warning sign

National Atomic Company Kazatomprom JSC (LSE:KAP)

Overview: National Atomic Company Kazatomprom JSC is a Kazakhstan based uranium producer that explores, mines, processes and sells uranium and related nuclear fuel products worldwide. It also produces rare metals like beryllium, tantalum and niobium and provides technical, security, logistics and consulting services across the nuclear value chain.

Operations: Kazatomprom generates most of its KZT 1,8 trillion in segment revenue from Uranium at KZT 1,576,005 million, with additional contributions from Other activities at KZT 283,371 million and Ulba Metallurgical Plant JSC at KZT 92,360 million, partly offset by KZT 208,872 million of eliminations.

Market Cap: US$18.2b

For investors looking at energy through an inflation lens, Kazatomprom sits in a different but important corner of the sector. It supplies uranium into long term nuclear contracts rather than selling oil or gas into spot markets. The company combines high reported profitability metrics, a large confirmed production and sales base for 2026, and a 2025 dividend payout that is set at 75% of free cash flow. At the same time, it faces rising input costs such as sulfuric acid, recent quarterly results that swung to a loss, and funding that relies on external borrowings. How those moving parts balance against its position as a leading uranium supplier tied to nuclear energy demand is a key consideration for investors.

High reported profitability and large contracted volumes are only part of Kazatomprom’s story. The 2 key rewards and 1 important warning sign helps you see how those strengths stack up against rising costs, recent losses and one underappreciated twist investors often miss.

data.title: One AI And Space Infrastructure Rack Star Three Stocks To Watch
data.description:
SpaceX’s record US$75b IPO and more than US$2tn debut valuation have grabbed headlines, but the real story for you is how this wave of enthusiasm for AI and space related technology could spill over into other stocks. With shares jumping nearly 20% on day one and over US$80b changing hands, risk appetite in this corner of the market looks very strong. This article highlights 3 stocks from an AI and space technology screener that appear especially exposed to this shift in attention, helping you decide which ideas might deserve a closer look or a place on your watchlist.

Wall Street's queuing for one rocket. While SpaceX counts down to its IPO, other companies tied to the new space race are already in orbit. → 20 Compelling Space Companies watchlist · Global Space Race Investing Ideas screener · Scan the sector by valuation on Rocket Lab's valuation page.

Advertisement

Extreme Networks (EXTR)

Overview: Extreme Networks provides AI powered cloud networking platforms that help enterprises and governments manage, secure, and automate wired and wireless networks across campuses, data centers, and remote sites.

Operations: The company generates about US$1.25b in revenue from developing and marketing network infrastructure equipment and related software, with sales spread across APAC, EMEA, the United States, and other Americas.

Market Cap: US$4.1b

Extreme Networks sits at the intersection of AI and critical network infrastructure, which is increasingly important as data heavy applications, including space and satellite services, rely on reliable, secure connectivity. The company is focusing on AI through its Platform ONE and Agent ONE products, aiming to expand recurring software and subscription revenue while supporting large customers such as governments and global enterprises. At the same time, investors need to weigh meaningful leverage, a recent non recurring loss of US$35.0m, and ongoing competition from much larger networking vendors. Extreme Networks may appeal to investors who want to examine an AI infrastructure company with recurring revenue streams and real world deployments and to assess how these strengths and risks align with their own objectives and risk tolerance.

Extreme Networks’ AI push and global customer base could be more powerful than the headline numbers suggest, but leverage and that US$35.0m loss matter. Weigh the upside and the pressure points in the 3 key rewards and 2 important warning signs

Lumentum Holdings (LITE)

Overview: Lumentum designs and manufactures lasers and optical components that move data inside cloud and AI data centers and power precision manufacturing tools in industries such as semiconductors, solar, displays, and electric vehicles. Its products sit inside the equipment used by large cloud operators, network gear makers, and industrial manufacturers that need fast, reliable data links and high performance lasers.

Operations: Lumentum generates hundreds of millions of US dollars in revenue from customers spread across Japan, Mexico, Thailand, Hong Kong, the United States, other Americas, other Asia Pacific markets, and EMEA, reflecting a globally diversified sales base.

Market Cap: US$69.2b

Lumentum operates at the intersection of AI and space themes, supplying the high end photonic components that help move data between GPU clusters on Earth and support satellite communications in orbit, a position that has drawn additional attention following the SpaceX IPO and renewed focus on AI driven space infrastructure. Recent results have included revenue and margin growth, Nvidia has committed US$2b to the company and broader photonics ecosystem, and analysts have revised their targets in response to AI data center demand and co packaged optics adoption. At the same time, the stock trades at a relatively high valuation and shows a heavy reliance on external funding, insider selling, and a record of earnings volatility, factors that make execution and future cash generation important considerations for anyone evaluating it.

AI and space fueled demand could be exciting for Lumentum, but the mix of high valuation, funding needs, and earnings swings is not straightforward, so it is worth reviewing the 4 key rewards and 2 important warning signs

Cerebras Systems (CBRS)

Overview: Cerebras Systems builds and sells AI computing racks for data centers and supercomputers, centered on its wafer scale engine chip that is designed to handle the heavy workloads of Generative AI, inference, and other advanced AI tasks faster than traditional GPUs. Its customers range from hyperscalers and AI labs to enterprises and Sovereign AI projects across the US and international markets.

Operations: Cerebras generates about US$510m in revenue primarily from semiconductors, with roughly US$188m from the United States, US$322m from Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, and a small contribution from other regions.

Market Cap: US$49.8b

Cerebras sits at the intersection of AI infrastructure and technology that can support AI driven broadband and satellite communications, an area currently drawing heightened attention from investors following the SpaceX IPO. The company has just turned profitable and has reported earnings and revenue growth, supported by its wafer scale engine, partnerships with groups like AWS and OpenAI, and a growing backlog of multi year AI inference contracts. At the same time, the balance sheet is aggressive, with negative equity, reliance on higher risk borrowing and highly illiquid shares, so funding and execution are key watchpoints. For investors looking at AI and space technology together, Cerebras presents a combination of growth characteristics and balance sheet risk that may warrant further research.

Cerebras’ wafer scale engine and fresh profitability could be masking an even bigger story for AI infrastructure growth and balance sheet risk, and the tension shows up clearly in the 4 key rewards and 2 important warning signs (1 is major!)

data.title: Callan JMB Insiders Still US$80k Away From Original Investment Value
data.description:
Insiders who purchased US$165.5k worth of Callan JMB Inc. (NASDAQ:CJMB) shares over the past year recouped some of their losses after price gained 14% last week. However, the purchase is proving to be a costly gamble, since losses made by insiders have totalled US$80k since the time of purchase.

While we would never suggest that investors should base their decisions solely on what the directors of a company have been doing, we would consider it foolish to ignore insider transactions altogether.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Callan JMB Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

In the last twelve months, the biggest single purchase by an insider was when Founder Wayne Williams bought US$51k worth of shares at a price of US$1.65 per share. That means that even when the share price was higher than US$0.82 (the recent price), an insider wanted to purchase shares. While their view may have changed since the purchase was made, this does at least suggest they have had confidence in the company's future. In our view, the price an insider pays for shares is very important. Generally speaking, it catches our eye when insiders have purchased shares at above current prices, as it suggests they believed the shares were worth buying, even at a higher price.

Callan JMB insiders may have bought shares in the last year, but they didn't sell any. You can see a visual depiction of insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last 12 months, below. If you click on the chart, you can see all the individual transactions, including the share price, individual, and the date!

View our latest analysis for Callan JMB

There are plenty of other companies that have insiders buying up shares. You probably do not want to miss this free list of undervalued small cap companies that insiders are buying.

Callan JMB Insiders Bought Stock Recently

Over the last three months, we've seen a bit of insider buying at Callan JMB. Chief Medical Officer David Croyle bought US$18k worth of shares in that time. We like it when there are only buyers, and no sellers. But in this case the amount purchased means the recent transaction may not be very meaningful on its own.

Insider Ownership

I like to look at how many shares insiders own in a company, to help inform my view of how aligned they are with insiders. Usually, the higher the insider ownership, the more likely it is that insiders will be incentivised to build the company for the long term. It's great to see that Callan JMB insiders own 57% of the company, worth about US$2.5m. This kind of significant ownership by insiders does generally increase the chance that the company is run in the interest of all shareholders.

So What Do The Callan JMB Insider Transactions Indicate?

Our data shows a little insider buying, but no selling, in the last three months. The net investment is not enough to encourage us much. However, our analysis of transactions over the last year is heartening. With high insider ownership and encouraging transactions, it seems like Callan JMB insiders think the business has merit. So while it's helpful to know what insiders are doing in terms of buying or selling, it's also helpful to know the risks that a particular company is facing. When we did our research, we found 4 warning signs for Callan JMB (2 are significant!) that we believe deserve your full attention.

Of course Callan JMB may not be the best stock to buy. So you may wish to see this free collection of high quality companies.

For the purposes of this article, insiders are those individuals who report their transactions to the relevant regulatory body. We currently account for open market transactions and private dispositions of direct interests only, but not derivative transactions or indirect interests.

Arch Resources (ARCH)

Operations: Arch Resources generates about US$1.6b from its Metallurgical segment and about US$1.1b from its Thermal segment, so revenue is currently weighted toward coal used in steel production.

Arch Resources sits in an interesting spot for value focused investors, with the stock trading on a relatively low P/E and the current price well below one estimate of fair value, while coal remains part of the global energy mix and a key input for steel. Alpha Metallurgical Resources (AMR)

Alpha Metallurgical Resources sits at the intersection of value and volatility, with a low P/E, strong balance sheet and a focus on premium met coal that fits the Value Stocks screener. Alpha’s low P/E, premium met coal focus and buybacks hint at a story investors may not be fully pricing. Get the full picture in the analysis report for Alpha Metallurgical Resources, where one capital allocation twist could change the script.

International Paper (IP)

International Paper gives value focused investors a large cap, cash oriented industrial that is currently loss making but priced on low multiples, with analysts expecting a turn back to profitability and margin improvement over the next few years. data.title: Tapestry (TPR) Stock After 89% Annual Gain Is Future Growth Already Priced In
data.description:
• Wondering if Tapestry stock still offers value after its recent run, or if expectations have already been priced in? This article breaks down what the current share price could mean for you.
  • The stock last closed at US$147.42, with returns of 5.2% over 7 days, 11.1% over 30 days, 14.2% year to date and 89.2% over 1 year, plus very large 3-year and 5-year gains of 265.6% and 307.6% respectively.
  • Recent attention on Tapestry has been shaped by ongoing coverage of the luxury sector and its role in consumer spending, which keeps investor focus on how resilient premium brands can be. Broader discussions about consumer confidence and discretionary spending trends also feed into how the market currently prices Tapestry stock.
  • Despite this strong share price history, Tapestry currently scores 0 out of 6 on Simply Wall St's valuation checks for potential undervaluation, giving it a value score of 0/6. Approach 1: Tapestry Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects the cash Tapestry could generate in the future and then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth in dollars right now.

Tapestry’s latest twelve month free cash flow is about $1.76b. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts supply explicit forecasts through 2028, with Simply Wall St extrapolating beyond that. For example, projected free cash flow for 2028 is $1.78b, and the model then extends out to 2035 with estimated figures such as $2.12b. Each year is discounted back to today using the chosen rate.

Adding these discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $144.78 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $147.42, the DCF output suggests Tapestry is around 1.8% above this model’s estimate. This indicates the stock is trading very close to the calculated fair value.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Tapestry is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Tapestry.

Approach 2: Tapestry Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Tapestry, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that share currently generates. In simple terms, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually support a higher, or more generous, P/E, while slower growth and higher risk tend to justify a lower multiple.

Tapestry is currently trading on a P/E of 44.94x. That sits well above both the Luxury industry average P/E of 24.44x and the peer group average of 24.26x. To give more context, Simply Wall St also calculates a “Fair Ratio” of 28.17x for Tapestry. This proprietary figure reflects factors such as earnings growth expectations, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the industry, because it is tailored to Tapestry’s own profile rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. Compared with the current 44.94x P/E, the Fair Ratio of 28.17x indicates that the stock is trading at a richer level than that model suggests.

Result: OVERVALUED
